proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_004 set to a higher ordinal level (range 0-2) have a lower probability of objective_response than those with lower levels.
Patients with feature_014=1 have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with feature_014=0.
Patients with feature_001=1 have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with feature_001=0.
Patients with feature_021=1 have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with feature_021=0.
Higher values of the continuous variable feature_020 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher values of the continuous variable feature_024 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import spearmanr; spearmanr(df['feature_004'], df['objective_response'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman correlation rho=-0.091 between feature_004 and objective_response (p=3.1e-92). Response rates by feature_004 level: 0=20.5%, 1=15.7%, 2=12.7%. Strong negative ordinal association.
Response rate is 23.5% in feature_014=1 (n=15,014) vs 16.7% in feature_014=0 (n=34,986). Risk difference +6.7 percentage points; chi-square p=5.8e-70.
Response rate is 22.1% in feature_001=1 (n=20,056) vs 16.5% in feature_001=0 (n=29,944). Risk difference +5.6 percentage points; chi-square p=1.9e-55.
Response rate is 22.0% in feature_021=1 (n=20,020) vs 16.6% in feature_021=0 (n=29,980). Risk difference +5.4 percentage points; chi-square p=6.6e-51.
Mean feature_020 is 3.44 in responders vs 3.95 in non-responders; Welch t-test p=1.7e-33. Higher feature_020 associated with lower response.
Mean feature_024 is 58.5 in responders vs 60.3 in non-responders; Welch t-test p=2.0e-16. Modest negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment, feature_004, feature_014, feature_001, feature_021, feature_020, feature_024, feature_026 and feature_009 each retain an independent association with objective_response.
Patients with feature_026=1 have a lower probability of objective_response than those with feature_026=0, independently of other covariates.
Patients with feature_009=1 have a lower probability of objective_response than those with feature_009=0, independently of other covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression of objective_response on all 35 features (continuous standardized). Significant independent predictors: feature_004 (beta=-0.36, p=9e-93), feature_014 (beta=+0.44, p=2e-71), feature_001 (beta=+0.37, p=2e-57), feature_021 (beta=+0.36, p=8e-54), feature_020 (beta=-0.14/SD, p=3e-31), feature_024 (beta=-0.10/SD, p=1e-17), feature_029 (beta=+0.06/SD, p=1e-7), feature_026 (beta=-0.17, p=6e-7), feature_009 (beta=-0.19, p=4e-6), feature_027 (beta=-0.06/SD, p=6e-6), feature_022 (beta=-0.05/SD, p=1e-4). The three large positive effects (014, 001, 021) and the moderate negative effects of feature_026 and feature_009 are particularly notable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014, feature_001 and feature_021 are roughly independently distributed in the cohort (pairwise correlations near zero), suggesting they represent separate biomarker or treatment indicators rather than a single mutually-exclusive treatment arm.
Within the cohort, the triple-positive subgroup (feature_014=1 AND feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1) has a higher objective_response rate than any other combination of these three features.

analyses.result_summary: Pairwise Pearson correlations among feature_014, feature_001, feature_021 are all <0.01 (statistically zero). Cell counts of all 8 combinations are consistent with marginal independence (e.g. ~30% have feature_014=1, ~40% have feature_001=1, joint cell ~12%). Confirms independent distribution.

Response rate by (feature_014, feature_001, feature_021) combination: (0,0,0)=17.2% n=12533; (0,0,1)=16.2% n=8383; (0,1,0)=16.4% n=8458; (0,1,1)=16.9% n=5612; (1,0,0)=16.0% n=5404; (1,0,1)=15.7% n=3624; (1,1,0)=16.0% n=3585; (1,1,1)=63.3% n=2401. Striking pattern: all 7 non-triple-positive combinations sit at ~16-17%, but the triple-positive cell jumps to 63.3%. Difference cannot be explained by additive main effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The associations of feature_014, feature_001 and feature_021 with objective_response are driven entirely by a positive three-way interaction term (feature_014 x feature_001 x feature_021), with the additive main effects becoming null after the interaction is included.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression with full three-way interaction: each main effect is null or slightly negative (feature_014 beta=-0.087 p=0.047; feature_001 beta=-0.052 p=0.17; feature_021 beta=-0.070 p=0.07). All two-way interactions are non-significant. The three-way interaction feature_014:feature_001:feature_021 has beta=+2.13 (z=20.4, p=2.5e-92), corresponding to OR=8.4 for the triple-positive cell beyond all marginal contributions. A simpler model with only the triple-product indicator gives beta=+2.17 (OR=8.7, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive subgroup (feature_014=1 AND feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1), the binary feature_026 has a strong negative association with objective_response that is much larger than its marginal effect in the full cohort.
Within the triple-positive subgroup, the binary feature_009 also has a strong negative association with objective_response that is much larger than its marginal effect in the full cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Within the n=2401 triple-positive subgroup, logistic regression shows feature_026 has beta=-2.78 (OR=0.062, p=5.5e-75) and feature_009 has beta=-2.67 (OR=0.069, p=1.1e-54). Both are an order of magnitude larger than their full-cohort effects (-0.17 and -0.19). Other features within this subgroup are mostly modest (feature_004 beta=-0.50, feature_020 beta=-0.18). The two suppressors stand out clearly.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 specifically modifies the triple-positive treatment effect: the positive triple-positive effect on objective_response is essentially abolished when feature_026=1 (significant negative interaction triple x feature_026).

feature_009 specifically modifies the triple-positive treatment effect: the positive triple-positive effect on objective_response is essentially abolished when feature_009=1 (significant negative interaction triple x feature_009).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction of triple x feature_026: triple beta=+2.55 (OR=12.8) when feature_026=0; interaction beta=-2.44 (z=-16.7, p=1.8e-62). Net effect of triple when feature_026=1: 2.55-2.44 = +0.11 (essentially null). Main effect of feature_026 in non-triple-positive: beta=+0.019, p=0.59 (i.e., feature_026 has no main effect outside the treatment context).

Interaction of triple x feature_009: triple beta=+2.42 (OR=11.3) when feature_009=0; interaction beta=-2.26 (z=-13.6, p=4.3e-42). Net effect of triple when feature_009=1: 2.42-2.26 = +0.16 (essentially null). Main effect of feature_009 in non-triple-positive: beta=-0.005, p=0.90 (no main effect outside treatment context).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete subgroup defined by feature_014=1 AND feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 has an objective_response rate substantially higher than every other combination of these five features in the cohort.

feature_026 and feature_009 act as parallel suppressors of the triple-positive treatment effect: the response collapse occurs as soon as either is positive, and presence of both does not produce additional suppression beyond having one.

analyses.result_summary: Triple-positive AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0: n=1790, response rate=78.6%. Triple-positive AND (feature_026=1 OR feature_009=1): n=611, response rate=18.5%. Not triple-positive: n=47,599, response rate=16.5%. Defining 'clean' = triple-positive AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0, logistic regression of objective_response on the clean indicator alone yields beta=+2.92 (OR=18.6, z=49.6, p<1e-300) — a much larger effect than any single-variable model.

Within triple-positive: feature_026=0,feature_009=0: rate=78.6% (n=1790); feature_026=0,feature_009=1: rate=18.7% (n=235); feature_026=1,feature_009=0: rate=18.2% (n=335); feature_026=1,feature_009=1: rate=19.5% (n=41). Either suppressor alone collapses response to background; having both does not reduce response further. This is consistent with parallel/redundant suppression rather than additive suppression.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other than feature_026 and feature_009, no other binary or continuous feature in the dataset materially modifies the triple-positive treatment effect on objective_response.

feature_024 is the only additional feature that interacts with the triple-positive indicator on objective_response (positive interaction: treatment effect somewhat stronger when feature_024 is high), but the magnitude is modest.

analyses.code: for f in features: smf.logit(f'objective_response ~ triple * Q("{f}")', data=df).fit().pvalues
analyses.result_summary: Systematic interaction screen of triple x each remaining feature on objective_response (35 tests). Top results by p-value: feature_026 (interaction beta=-2.44, p=1.8e-62); feature_009 (-2.26, p=4.3e-42); feature_024 (+0.15/SD, p=5.6e-4); all remaining features have p>0.10. After Bonferroni correction (alpha=0.0014 for 35 tests), only feature_026, feature_009, and feature_024 retain significance — and only the first two have effects large enough to alter clinical decisions.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the 'clean' subgroup (triple-positive AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0), the ordinal feature_004 retains a strong negative gradient on objective_response, but does not abolish the treatment benefit (response rate stays above 65% even at feature_004=2).

feature_004 acts primarily as a general prognostic factor rather than a true treatment-effect modifier: it affects response rates similarly inside and outside the clean subgroup, with only a borderline interaction term.

analyses.result_summary: Within the clean subgroup (n=1790): feature_004=0: response 86.0% (n=617); feature_004=1: 77.2% (n=902); feature_004=2: 66.8% (n=271). Logistic regression beta=-0.56 per unit (p=1e-10). Even at the worst level (feature_004=2), response remains ~67%, far above the 16.5% background. So feature_004 attenuates but does not abolish the treatment benefit.

Whole-cohort interaction model: clean main effect beta=+3.13 (p<1e-300); feature_004 main effect beta=-0.38 (p<1e-89); interaction clean:feature_004 beta=-0.17 (p=0.050, borderline). Response by feature_004 outside clean: 0=20.8%, 1=15.2%, 2=10.9% — same negative gradient as inside clean. Consistent with feature_004 being a general prognostic factor whose absolute effect is somewhat amplified inside the responding subgroup, but not a true effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete and final treatment-effect subgroup definition for objective_response in this cohort is: feature_014=1 AND feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0. Patients meeting this definition have a response rate of approximately 79%, compared with approximately 17% for any patient missing one or more of these five conditions; this corresponds to an odds ratio of about 18-19 and a 62 percentage-point absolute risk difference.

Conditional on feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0, the triple-positive indicator (feature_014 x feature_001 x feature_021) is itself the active treatment-defining variable: in this stratum the triple has OR ~18.7 vs not.

analyses.result_summary: Final subgroup model: clean (=1 if feature_014=1 AND feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0): beta=+2.921, OR=18.55, z=49.57, p<<1e-300. Group means: clean=1: 78.60% response (n=1790); clean=0: 16.53% response (n=48,210). Risk difference 62.07 percentage points.

Restricting to feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 (n=42,890), logistic regression of objective_response on triple-product indicator (feature_014*feature_001*feature_021) yields beta=+2.926 (OR=18.65, z=49.31, p<<1e-300). Identical OR and significance to the 'clean' indicator from the full cohort, confirming the multiplicative structure: triple is the active treatment-like variable; feature_026 and feature_009 are gating prerequisites that completely abolish the effect when present.
